sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal.

Piel שֵׁרֵת inf. שָׁרֵת and with the tone drawn back, שָׁרֶת Deu 17:12 fut. convers. וַיְשָׁ֫רֶת to wait upon, to serve, to minister unto, with an acc. of pers. Gen 39:4 40:4 Num 3:6 1Ki 1:15 followed by לְ Num 4:9. There often occurs, שֵׁרֵת אֶת־יְיָ concerning the ministering priests, Num 18:2 1Sa 2:11 3:1 and without the acc., Num 3:31 4:12 from which we must distinguish שֵׁרֵת בְּשֵׁם יְיָ Deu 18:5, Deu 18:7, i.e. to worship Jehovah by calling upon him, according to the analogy of the phrases קָרָא בְּשֵׁם יְיָ, בֵּרֵךְ בְּשֵׁם יְיָ. A very bold expression, Isa 60:7, “the rams of Nebaioth יְשָֽׁרְתוּנֶךְ shall minister to thee,” i.e. shall serve for sacred ministry. Part. מְשָׁרֵת subst. a minister, a servant, Jos 1:1 specially in holy things, Ezr 8:17 fem. מְשָׁרַת (for מְשָׁרֶתֶת), 1Ki 1:15.
